ogTitle: Gillieston Heights vs Gilmandyke: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gillieston Heights and Gilmandyke.
heading: Gillieston Heights vs Gilmandyke — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gillieston Heights, New South Wales and Gilmandyke, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gillieston Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Gilmandyke (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gillieston Heights or Gilmandyke safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gillieston Heights has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Gilmandyke scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gillieston Heights scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Gilmandyke scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gillieston Heights or Gilmandyke?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gillieston Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
